title: Personal Loan Payment Table — Compare Monthly Payments by Amount & Rate | QFINHUB
h1: Personal Loan Payment Tables — Monthly Payments at a Glance
meta_description: Quick-reference loan payment tables for personal loans from $5,000 to $30,000. Compare monthly payments across different rates and terms. Free, no signup required.

content.intro: These tables show estimated monthly payments for common personal loan amounts, rates, and terms. Use these as a quick reference — for your exact numbers, try our free Loan Calculator with your specific amount, rate, and term.
content.methodology: All payments calculated using the standard amortization formula: Monthly Payment = P × [r(1+r)^n] / [(1+r)^n - 1], where P = principal, r = monthly interest rate (APR/12), n = total number of payments (term × 12). Assumes fixed rate, no fees, no prepayment penalties. Actual loan terms may differ.

faq.q: Why do different loan amounts have different rates?
faq.a: Smaller loans (under $10K) often carry higher rates because lenders have minimum processing costs. Larger loans ($20K+) can offer lower rates because the lender earns more total interest. Your credit score is the biggest factor — excellent credit can get 7-10%, while fair credit may see 15-25%.

faq.q: How accurate are these payment estimates?
faq.a: These use standard amortization math and are mathematically correct for the stated inputs. However, your actual loan may include origination fees (1-8% of the loan), prepayment penalties, or variable rates — none of which are reflected here. Always get the full APR (which includes fees) from your lender.

faq.q: Which loan scenario is right for me?
faq.a: Click through to each scenario page for detailed guidance. Generally: (1) Emergency loans — borrow only what you need, (2) Good credit — you have the most options and lowest rates, (3) Consolidation — only if you'll save vs current debt, (4) Fair credit — consider improving credit first if possible.

content.disclaimer: These are educational estimates, not loan offers. Actual rates depend on your credit profile, income, and lender. APR shown is interest rate only and may not include origination fees. Always compare the full APR from multiple lenders before borrowing.
